mnemonics: TD: Think 'Derivation'. BD: Build 'Derivation' from the leaves up.
Top-Down: Starts from the top (root) and drills down. Bottom-Up: Starts from the bottom (leaves) and builds up.
keyPoints: Top-down parsing begins with the start symbol and applies production rules to reach the input string.
Bottom-up parsing begins with the input string and applies production rules in reverse to reduce it to the start symbol.
Recursive Descent is a common top-down technique, while Shift-Reduce parsing is a common bottom-up technique.
commonMistakes: Confusing the direction of parsing: Top-down expands, Bottom-up reduces.
Assuming both techniques are equally efficient for all grammars; Top-down can struggle with left-recursion, while Bottom-up is more general but can be complex.
